array_semantics.argmax_mode_u8: factor-by-factor block mode of parent argmax_u8; equal-count ties choose the lower class index
array_semantics.boundary_coverage_u8: rounded block mean of parent boundary_u8 mapped from [0,1] to [0,255]
array_semantics.flip_risk_log_mean_u8: rounded block mean after global log1p normalization of the historical compatibility-named parent flip_risk_f32; this field is a margin-normalized summed-margin sensitivity proxy, not a probability
array_semantics.gradient_log_mean_u8: rounded block mean after global log1p normalization of parent gradient_magnitude_f32

downsample.boundary_reduce: sum binary boundary block values, scale the block mean from [0,1] to [0,255], then apply positive integer half-up rounding
downsample.continuous_reduce: sum uint16 block values, scale the block mean from [0,65535] to [0,255], then apply positive integer half-up rounding
downsample.source_only: locked parent NPZ; no scorer, video decode, or Pact checkout consulted

limitations: One fixed real pair only; this is not an n600 population estimate.
macOS CPU is advisory and is not the contest-CPU or contest-CUDA authority axis.
The surface is a heuristic pixel-space margin-normalized summed-margin sensitivity proxy, not a local flip radius and not STAC's DCT-coefficient gradient.
No quantized reconstruction, actual byte measurement, optical flow, or key-frame experiment is included.
No comma.ai score, improvement, ranking, promotion, or exact-eval claim is made.
This factor-reduced, 8-bit display derivative is lossy and is not a measurement surface.
Block-mode labels and block-mean log fields are for public rendering only; use the locked parent for analysis.
The derivative creates no score, STAC, rank, promotion, or exact-eval authority.
